lead:
**LocalPulse** is a local-first event discovery and organiser platform I built with **Next.js 15 App Router**, React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui. I built it in March 2026 — it's the most recent web project in my portfolio. It uses local browser storage as a zero-cost database backend alongside Firebase, and is built as a **Progressive Web App** with offline support and an install prompt.

LocalPulse demonstrates a full-stack TypeScript application with authentication (Clerk), file-based routing, PDF ticket generation, calendar export (.ics), and an admin panel — all in a modern serverless-ready architecture. Code is on [GitHub](https://github.com/lingadevaru-hp/LocalPulse).

rows.What I used it for: File-based routing, server and client components, layouts
Component library built on Radix UI primitives
End-to-end type safety — typed API routes, typed React components, typed Firebase calls
Sign in, sign up, profile, password reset — managed auth so I don't roll my own
Zero-cost database — no backend server required
Install prompt, mobile icons, offline-capable pages

content.content:
**Event Discovery:** I built search and filter by city, category, and date. Featured events section. Smart recommendations using category + city + rating affinity scoring. Access-controlled event visibility: local, college, department.

**Organiser Workflow:** Organiser verification via document upload (Aadhaar, PAN, GST, business docs). Status states: pending / approved / rejected. Admin review panel.

**Ticketing:** Downloadable PDF tickets named by event. Ticket history per user. Reminder toggles. Add-to-calendar export (.ics — Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple Calendar).

**Local Data Hub (/database):** Inspect all locally stored data, export backup JSON, import data on another device, clear all data. All of this runs without a server — the user's browser is the database.

content.title: Why zero-cost architecture matters to me
content.content: Using local browser storage as the primary database means no database hosting costs. Firebase supplements this for features requiring server-side persistence. The entire platform runs at near-zero cost — important when I'm shipping personal projects without a budget.

rows.Purpose: User ticket history and PDF download
Admin control panel — organiser approval, analytics
Local Data Hub — inspect, export, import, clear

sections.title: How This Fits Into My Work

content.content:
LocalPulse contrasts sharply with my [[fraud-detection|Fraud Detection System]] in approach: where fraud detection used a traditional server-side stack (ASP.NET, SQL Server), LocalPulse embraces the modern serverless React ecosystem (Next.js App Router, Clerk managed auth, Firebase, edge deployment).

The project demonstrates TypeScript across a full-stack application — type-safe API routes, typed React components, typed Firebase calls — rather than the JavaScript-without-types approach common in quick prototypes. It's the closest thing I have to a 'standard' full-stack web project, compared to my blockchain and infrastructure work.
